tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I wanted to start tracking information about a college player I'm mentoring. His name is Marcus Thompson, he's a wide receiver, probably a 7 on skill level right now. He's got good speed and route-running but needs work on contested catches. For training, he prefers morning sessions and wants to focus on building his strength and improving his footwork. Can you help me store this so I can reference it later?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "College player profile - Marcus Thompson: Wide Receiver, skill level rated 7/10. Strengths: good speed, route-running. Areas for improvement: contested catches. Training preferences: morning sessions. Training focus areas: building strength and improving footwork."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "College player profile - Marcus Thompson: Wide Receiver, skill level rated 7/10. Strengths: good speed, route-running. Areas for improvement: contested catches. Training preferences: morning sessions. Training focus areas: building strength and improving footwork."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've stored Marcus Thompson's player profile with all the details:

- **Position:** Wide Receiver
- **Skill Level:** 7/10
- **Strengths:** Good speed, route-running
- **Areas for Improvement:** Contested catches
- **Training Preferences:** Morning sessions
- **Focus Areas:** Building strength and improving footwork

You can reference this anytime—just let me know and I'll pull it up for you.

Now that I have his basic profile stored, I'd like to find more specific information about Marcus's performance patterns. Can you help me look up any details about his game footage, statistics from past games, or notes on specific situations where he struggles with contested catches? That way I can personalize his coaching plan better.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 5, "query": "Marcus Thompson wide receiver game footage statistics performance contested catches"}

messages.content: {"results": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "College player profile - Marcus Thompson: Wide Receiver, skill level rated 7/10. Strengths: good speed, route-running. Areas for improvement: contested catches. Training preferences: morning sessions. Training focus areas: building strength and improving footwork."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
I searched for additional details about Marcus's performance, but I only found the basic profile we just stored. There's no game footage data, game statistics, or detailed notes on his contested catch struggles currently saved.

It looks like this is new information that hasn't been added yet. Would you like to:

1. **Add more detailed notes now** – If you have specific game stats, footage analysis, or observations about situations where he struggles with contested catches, I can store those for you.
2. **Search again later** – Once you add more detailed information, I can help you search and organize it.

What details do you have that you'd like to store?
Now I'd like to review everything we've stored so far about Marcus. Can you show me all the information that's currently saved about him so I can verify it's all correct?

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "College player profile - Marcus Thompson: Wide Receiver, skill level rated 7/10. Strengths: good speed, route-running. Areas for improvement: contested catches. Training preferences: morning sessions. Training focus areas: building strength and improving footwork."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's everything currently stored about Marcus:

---

**Player Profile (ID: m2)**
- **Name:** Marcus Thompson
- **Position:** Wide Receiver
- **Skill Level:** 7/10
- **Strengths:** Good speed, route-running
- **Areas for Improvement:** Contested catches
- **Training Preferences:** Morning sessions
- **Focus Areas:** Building strength and improving footwork

---

That's the complete set of information currently saved. Does everything look correct, or would you like to add or modify any details?
Everything looks correct to me. I don't see any outdated or incorrect information that needs to be deleted. The profile accurately reflects what we discussed, so we're good there.

Got it! Marcus's profile is all set and accurately stored. If you need to add game stats, performance notes, or any other details down the road, just let me know—I'm here to help you build out his coaching plan.
